skill.name: string-database
skill.description: Access the STRING database to map identifiers, retrieve protein–protein interaction networks, and run functional/PPI enrichment when you need interaction context for a gene/protein set.
skill.summary: Query the STRING protein interaction database to map gene IDs, retrieve PPI networks, run enrichment analysis, and download network images.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill wraps the official STRING database REST API (string-db.org) for protein-protein interaction analysis. The static analyzer flagged 47 potential issues, but all high-severity findings (weak cryptographic algorithms, system reconnaissance) and all medium-severity findings (external commands, backtick execution) are FALSE POSITIVES in context. The code contains no cryptographic operations and all backtick usage is markdown code formatting in documentation. Network calls target only the legitimate STRING API at string-db.org. One filesystem write operation is expected behavior for saving network images. Risk level is LOW and the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick-enclosed text in markdown files as Ruby/shell command execution. These are markdown code formatting backticks in documentation files (SKILL.md and references/string_reference.md), not actual shell commands. The code blocks show Python usage examples for the STRING API client.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backtick occurrences are inside markdown code fences or inline code spans showing Python example code. No shell execution context exists.

low_findings.title: Network Calls to STRING Database API
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged HTTP requests via Python requests library. All calls target version-12-0.string-db.org/api, which is the official STRING database REST API. No data exfiltration or unauthorized endpoint is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every network call uses the BASE_URL https://version-12-0.string-db.org/api. This is the well-documented, legitimate STRING database API used in bioinformatics.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write for Network Image Saving
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged open() with write mode in get_network_image method. This writes the downloaded STRING network image (PNG) to a user-specified file path. This is the expected behavior for the image download feature.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem write is intrinsic to the skill's stated image download feature. The output_file parameter is user-controlled and the method documentation explicitly states it saves the network image.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines for weak cryptographic algorithms, but no cryptographic operations exist in any source file. The skill does not implement encryption, hashing, or any cryptographic functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: After reading all source files, no cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, AES, etc.) were found. This is a pattern-matching false positive.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged debug print statements as system reconnaissance. These are commented-out debug lines and standard Python print calls in example code, not information gathering operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 197 in string_api.py is commented out (# print). Line 50 in SKILL.md is a print statement in example code showing standard output. Neither performs reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Analyze Protein-Protein Interactions via STRING Database
content.value_statement: Researchers waste time learning complex APIs to query the STRING protein interaction database. This skill provides a simple Python client that maps gene identifiers, retrieves PPI networks, runs functional enrichment analysis, and downloads network images in a few lines of code.
actual_capabilities: Map gene and protein identifiers to STRING database IDs for any organism using NCBI taxonomy ID
Retrieve protein-protein interaction networks with configurable confidence scores
Run functional enrichment analysis including GO terms, KEGG pathways, and Reactome pathways
Calculate PPI enrichment statistics for a protein set
Find interaction partners for target proteins to expand candidate lists
Download static network visualizations as PNG images with evidence coloring
limitations: Requires internet access to query the STRING API at string-db.org
Does not include local caching for repeated queries
Cannot perform sequence alignment or homology modeling
Large gene lists (over 1000 IDs) may cause API timeouts

use_cases.title: Find Interaction Partners for Drug Target Research
use_cases.description: A pharmacologist has a target protein and needs to discover its top interaction partners to understand potential off-target effects and downstream signaling. Use the skill to map the protein ID and retrieve interaction partners with confidence scores.

use_cases.title: Functional Enrichment for Gene List Interpretation
use_cases.description: A biologist has a list of differentially expressed genes from an RNA-seq experiment and needs to understand which biological pathways are over-represented. Use the skill to map identifiers and run GO and KEGG enrichment analysis.

use_cases.title: Generate PPI Network Figures for Publications
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs a network visualization of their protein set for a conference poster or journal article. Use the skill to retrieve the interaction network and save a high-quality PNG image.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Gene Name to a STRING Protein ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the human gene TP53 to its STRING identifier using the string-database skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a gene symbol and need the STRING protein ID for further downstream analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve Protein Interaction Network with Confidence Filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the string-database skill to get the protein-protein interaction network for these human proteins: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR. Set the confidence threshold to 700 for high confidence interactions only.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a list of proteins and want to see their known high-confidence interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Run Functional Enrichment on a Protein Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Run functional enrichment analysis through the string-database skill for these STRING IDs: 9606.ENSP00000269305, 9606.ENSP00000418963. Use species 9606 for human.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a set of proteins and need to discover which biological pathways they participate in.

prompt_templates.title: Expand a Seed Protein List by Adding Network Neighbors
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the string-database skill, map the gene BRCA1 for human species, then find its top 15 interaction partners and save the network image to brca1_network.png with additional nodes colored.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a seed protein and want to discover new candidate genes by expanding the network.

output_examples.input: Map the human gene TP53 to a STRING identifier.
Get interaction network for TP53, BRCA1, and EGFR at high confidence (700).
output_examples.output: Retrieved 12 interaction edges between the 3 proteins. Confidence scores range from 0.701 to 0.999. Interactions include evidence from curated databases, experiments, and co-expression data.

best_practices: Always specify the species parameter using the NCBI taxonomy ID to ensure accurate results for your organism of interest
Use a meaningful caller_identity string (project name or email) to help STRING track API usage and maintain fair access
Start with a confidence threshold of 400 for medium confidence and increase to 700 for high confidence if results are too noisy
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without specifying a species parameter, as results may be incorrect for queries spanning multiple organisms
Do not pass very large gene lists in a single API call, as STRING may time out or return incomplete results for lists over 1000 IDs
Do not rely on the network image output for quantitative analysis, use the TSV data output instead to get precise confidence scores

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use the STRING database skill?
faq.answer: No. STRING does not require an API key. You only need to provide a caller_identity string (such as a project name or email) for rate management purposes.

faq.question: Which organisms are supported by this skill?
faq.answer: STRING covers over 5000 organisms. You specify the organism using its NCBI taxonomy ID. The most common ID is 9606 for human.

faq.question: What is the meaning of the confidence score?
faq.answer: Confidence scores range from 0 to 1000. A score of 400 is medium confidence, 700 is high confidence, and 900 is very high confidence.

faq.question: Can I save a visual image of the interaction network?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can download a static network image (PNG format) from STRING with color-coded evidence lines showing different types of interaction evidence.

faq.question: What types of enrichment analysis are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports functional enrichment including Gene Ontology (GO) terms, KEGG pathways, Reactome pathways, PFAM domains, and local network cluster enrichment.

faq.question: Does this skill work without internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires an active internet connection to query the STRING REST API hosted at string-db.org.
